originalTitle: Women secretly filmed, then ridiculed and abused online
title: Let's Be Safe and Kind Online: A Chat About Our Videos!
content:
Hello, Wonderful Friends!

Hi everyone! I'm your friendly news reporter, here to share important stories from our amazing home, Africa! Today, we're going to talk about something very important. It's about being kind and safe when we use computers or phones to watch things or share pictures. Are you ready?

What Happened to Some Ladies?

Imagine you are walking around, happy and smiling. Maybe you are at the market, or just walking near your home. Then, a person comes to you. They talk to you. Maybe they ask your name, or something simple like that. But then, something not so good happens. This person *secretly* takes a video of you. They don't ask you if it's okay. They just do it, maybe with their phone, without you knowing. This happened to some lovely ladies in countries like Kenya and Ghana. These countries are part of our big, beautiful African family! A man filmed them without asking for their permission. Did you know? Kenya and Ghana are wonderful countries in Africa, full of amazing people, special animals, and beautiful places like sandy beaches and green forests! They are far from each other but both in Africa. Then, after filming them secretly, this man put the videos on the internet. The internet is like a very, very big place where many, many people can see things. And these ladies did not want their videos to be seen by everyone. It made them feel very sad, worried, and even a little scared.

What is 'Consent'?

This is a big word, but it means something super simple and very important. 'Consent' means asking for permission. It means saying 'yes' or 'no' if you want something to happen to you or your things. For example, if your friend wants to play with your favourite toy, they should always ask you, 'Can I play with your toy?' You can say 'yes' if you want to share, or 'no' if you don't. That's consent! You get to decide. It's the same with taking pictures or videos of someone. You must always, always ask first! You can say, 'May I take your picture?' or 'Is it okay if I film this?' If someone says 'no', you must respect that. You must not take their picture or video if they say no. It's polite and kind to ask. Everyone has the right to say 'yes' or 'no' about their own picture or video.

Why is This Not Good?

When someone takes your video without asking, it's not fair. It's like they are taking something that belongs to you – your face, your smile, your special moment – without your permission. It's a bit like someone taking your drawing without asking you first. And when they put it online, on the internet, many, many people can see it. Sometimes, people online are not kind. They might say mean things or laugh at people in the videos. This is called 'ridiculing' or 'abusing' someone online. It makes people feel very, very bad, sad, and sometimes even angry. We all have a right to feel safe. And we have a right to decide who sees our pictures and videos. Our faces and our stories are special, and we get to choose who we share them with.

What Happens Online?

The internet is like a very, very big library or a huge playground for information. You can learn many amazing things, watch fun videos, and sometimes even talk to friends and family who live far away. But just like in a real playground, there are rules to keep everyone safe and happy. And sometimes, there are people who don't follow the rules. They might do things that are not kind or safe, like sharing videos without asking. Did you know? The internet connects computers and phones all over the world! It's like a giant spiderweb that links places between Kenya, Ghana, and even where you are right now! That's why it's so important to be careful and kind online.

Keeping Ourselves Safe

So, what can we do to keep safe? First, always remember to ask before you take a picture or video of someone. It's the kind and right thing to do. And if someone ever tries to take a picture or video of you, it's okay to say, 'Please don't do that,' or 'Please ask me first.' You have a voice, and you can use it to protect yourself. Also, be careful what you share online. Once something is on the internet, it's very hard to make it disappear. It's like putting a message in a bottle and throwing it into the big, wide ocean! Many people might see it.

What Can We Do?

If someone ever tries to film you without asking, or if you see something online that makes you feel bad or uncomfortable, what should you do? It's really, really important to know this step. The most important thing is to tell a trusted grown-up. This could be your mom, your dad, a teacher, an auntie, an uncle, or any elder who you trust. They are there to help you and keep you safe. Did you know? Your grown-ups love you very much and want to keep you safe and happy. They are your best helpers when something feels wrong or scary!

Talking to Grown-ups

It's never, ever wrong to tell a grown-up if something makes you feel uncomfortable or sad, especially when it happens with pictures or videos, or online. They can help you understand what's happening and what to do next. They can also help to make sure the mean person stops sharing videos that are not allowed. Grown-ups know how to ask for help from bigger helpers, like the police, if needed.

Being a Good Friend Online

Remember, let's always be kind. Not just when we are playing together in person, but also when we are online. Don't share pictures or videos of your friends or anyone else without asking them first. Always get their consent! If you see someone being mean online, or sharing things that make others sad, speak up or tell a trusted grown-up. Let's make the internet a happy and safe place for everyone. This way, our friends in Kenya and Ghana, and all over Africa and the world, can feel safe and happy too! Thank you for listening, my wonderful friends! See you next time, and remember to be kind and safe, always!
summary: Some women in Kenya and Ghana had their videos secretly taken and shared online without their permission, making them sad, which teaches us why it's super important to always ask 'yes' or 'no' before sharing anything about others.
whyMatters: This story matters because everyone has a right to feel safe and decide if pictures or videos of them go on the internet, and it teaches us to always be kind, ask for permission, and tell a grown-up if something bad happens online.

originalTitle: South Africa's exclusion from G7 summit no surprise, says Ramaphosa
title: Oh no! President Cyril Missed a Big Meeting!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your kid news reporter here, bringing you super important news from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we have a story about our friend, President Cyril Ramaphosa from South Africa. It's about a big meeting he was going to attend, but then... he didn't! Let's find out what happened. Don't worry, we'll make it super easy to understand! It's like when you get invited to a birthday party, but then something changes.

What is the G7 Meeting?

Imagine a club for the leaders of some very big and powerful countries. They meet to talk about important things that affect everyone in the world. Things like making sure everyone has enough food, taking care of our planet, and how countries can work together. This special club is called the G7. G7 stands for "Group of Seven" because there are seven main countries in it. They are Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, the United Kingdom, and the United States. They are like the "big kids" on the world playground! Did you know? The G7 leaders often meet in a different country each year! It's a big event!

Who is President Cyril Ramaphosa?

President Cyril Ramaphosa is the leader of a beautiful country called South Africa. South Africa is right here on our continent, Africa! President Ramaphosa works very hard to make South Africa a better place for all its people. He also helps Africa talk to other big countries around the world. He's a very important person for us in Africa. Did you know? South Africa has 11 official languages! That's a lot of ways to say hello!

A Special Invitation!

Guess what? The leader of France, who was hosting the G7 meeting that year, decided to invite President Ramaphosa! It was a very special invitation. It meant that South Africa and the whole of Africa would have a voice at this big, important table. President Ramaphosa was happy to be asked to join the discussion. It's like being invited to play with the older kids, and they want to hear your ideas! This invitation was a big deal for Africa. It showed that African countries are important. It showed that our ideas matter. President Ramaphosa was ready to go and represent us.

Uh Oh! Invitation Taken Back!

But then, something unexpected happened. The invitation that President Ramaphosa received was taken back. Poof! It was like getting a party invitation, and then someone says, "Actually, maybe not." This is called "rescinding an invitation." It means it's cancelled. So, President Ramaphosa could not go to the G7 meeting after all. He didn't get to sit with the other big leaders that time. Imagine how you might feel if that happened to you! It can be a little confusing and maybe a bit sad.

Why Did This Happen?

So, why did the invitation get taken back? Well, it's a bit like when friends don't agree. Reports say that the President of the United States at that time, whose name was Donald Trump, was not happy about President Ramaphosa being invited. He said that if President Ramaphosa came, he would not come to the meeting himself. This is called "threatening to boycott." It means he wouldn't show up. Because of this, it's reported that the United States talked to France. They put "pressure" on France. This means they strongly asked France to uninvite President Ramaphosa. So, France, which was hosting the meeting, decided to take back the invitation. It was a tough choice for France to make. They wanted everyone to be happy, but it didn't quite work out that way. Did you know? Countries often talk to each other to solve problems. Sometimes they don't agree!

How Did President Ramaphosa Feel?

When President Ramaphosa found out, he said he was "not surprised." That means he kind of expected it might happen. He probably understood that sometimes these big country meetings can be tricky. He knows that countries have different ideas and sometimes those ideas bump into each other. He wasn't shocked, but he still wished he could have gone to represent Africa. He also knows that South Africa is strong and important, no matter what. He said that South Africa will always try to work with other countries. He wants peace and friendship for everyone.

Why is This Important for South Africa?

Even though President Ramaphosa didn't go, this story is still important. It shows that South Africa and other African countries are very important in the world. We have a lot to offer. We have smart people, amazing animals, and beautiful places. Our voices need to be heard at these big meetings. When an African leader is invited, it helps tell the world about our continent's needs and ideas. It helps us work together to solve big problems like climate change or poverty. It's important that all voices are heard, especially voices from places like Africa, which is home to so many wonderful people.

What Happens Next?

Even though President Ramaphosa didn't go to that G7 meeting, South Africa and Africa will keep working hard. They will keep talking to other countries. They will keep making friends. Our leaders will find other ways to share Africa's message with the world. We are strong, and we are important! And one day, African leaders will surely be at all the big tables, sharing their wisdom. We will keep you updated on all the news from our amazing continent! See you next time, news explorers!
summary: President Cyril Ramaphosa of South Africa was invited to a big meeting called the G7, but his invitation was taken back after another country's leader didn't want him there.
whyMatters: It matters because Africa's voice is important, and our leaders should be included in big talks about the world.
